name: Act 1St
description_short: ACT 1st Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, NCUA-insured credit union serving Maryland and West Virginia with checking, savings, loans, and financial services.
description_long:
ACT 1st Federal Credit Union is a federally-chartered credit union operating in the Cumberland and LaVale areas of Maryland, with an additional branch in Romney, West Virginia. The institution is NCUA-insured and operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned model typical of federal credit unions. The organization emphasizes community involvement and member growth, as evidenced by their tagline 'We're growing and we're taking you with us.' ACT 1st FCU holds NMLS #654273 and maintains routing number 252176889.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal checking accounts, various savings vehicles (savings accounts, share certificates, youth accounts, and club accounts), consumer and business VISA credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, home equity lines of credit, and student loan refinancing. Members also have access to digital banking services including online and mobile banking, bill pay, remote deposit, account alerts, and mobile wallet functionality. The institution partners with third-party providers for insurance products (TruStage, AFLAC, and Global Life insurance) and financial wellness services (GreenPath Financial Wellness).

ACT 1st FCU distinguishes itself through several member-focused programs not universally available at all credit unions: the uChoose Rewards program for debit card benefits, a Loan Payment Skip Program for auto and personal loans, a First Time Car Buyer Program specifically designed for new borrowers, and a Summer Savers Account targeted at school employees. The credit union also provides loan payment protection services and maintains a robust ATM network with recent expansions planned for 2026. Their commitment to financial education is reflected in offered workshops, webinars, financial calculators, and credit score monitoring tools.

ACT 1st FCU operates as a traditional, well-established credit union with solid fundamentals and community ties. However, membership is restricted to those meeting specific eligibility criteria (not stated on accessible pages), and their service area is geographically limited to Maryland and West Virginia. The institution appears operationally sound with federal insurance protection, though as a smaller regional credit union, they may lack the technology sophistication or product breadth of larger national institutions.
services: Youth accounts and club savings accounts
Consumer VISA credit cards with uChoose Rewards
Auto loans including First Time Car Buyer Program
Personal loans with Loan Payment Skip option
Mortgages and Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs)
Online and mobile banking with bill pay
TruStage, AFLAC, and Global Life insurance products
Financial calculators and credit score monitoring
Debit and credit cards with 24/7 lost/stolen support
pros: NCUA-insured federal credit union providing deposit security up to $250,000
uChoose Rewards program offers cashback and benefits on debit card purchases
Loan Payment Skip Program allows borrowers to defer auto and personal loan payments when needed
First Time Car Buyer Program designed specifically for those with limited credit history
Multiple branches across Maryland (LaVale, Cumberland) and West Virginia (Romney) for in-person service
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile banking, bill pay, and remote deposit
Member-focused financial wellness services including GreenPath Financial Wellness and educational workshops
Summer Savers Club Account specifically for school employees with irregular income
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not transparently listed on website, creating barrier to access for unknown populations
Limited geographic footprint with only three branches constrains convenience for members outside Maryland/West Virginia service area
Technology and product offerings appear more limited compared to large national credit unions or banks
Website lacks rate transparency—specific APR/APY figures for individual products not displayed on main pages
Smaller institution size may mean fewer advanced financial products or services compared to mega-institutions
best_for: School employees seeking paycheck-based savings solutions through the Summer Savers Account
First-time car buyers with limited credit history who benefit from dedicated auto loan programs
Residents of Cumberland, LaVale, Maryland and Romney, West Virginia seeking local banking relationships
Members prioritizing community involvement and not-for-profit institutional values over maximum features
diagnosis: ACT 1st Federal Credit Union is best for members within their Maryland and West Virginia service areas who value community-focused, not-for-profit banking with specialized programs like first-time buyer auto loans and school employee savings clubs. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—membership benefits are contingent on location eligibility and physical proximity to one of three branches, making this unsuitable for those outside the service area or requiring nationwide accessibility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
